findings.name: IDOR — API Resource Access
findings.summary: Horizontal IDOR on /api/Users/{id} and /rest/basket/{id} — any authenticated user can access other users' data by changing the ID parameter.
findings.evidence: GET /api/Users/1 returned admin@juice-sh.op profile. GET /rest/basket/2 returned another user's cart.

findings.name: JWT & Session Token Analysis
findings.summary: JWT uses weak HS256 secret. Algorithm confusion attack succeeds — setting alg to 'none' bypasses signature verification. Token does not expire.
findings.evidence: Modified JWT with alg:'none' accepted by /api/Users endpoint. No exp claim in token.

findings.name: SSRF — Server-Side Request Forgery
findings.summary: No URL input fields found in standard user flows. Profile image uses file upload, not URL fetch.
findings.evidence: Tested /profile/image endpoint — only accepts file uploads, not URLs.

findings.name: Mass Assignment & Parameter Pollution
findings.summary: PUT /api/Users/{id} accepts 'role' field — setting role to 'admin' grants admin privileges.
findings.evidence: PUT /api/Users/22 with {"role":"admin"} — subsequent requests show admin panel accessible.

findings.name: Security Headers & CSP Audit
findings.summary: Missing critical headers: no CSP, no X-Frame-Options, X-Powered-By exposes Express version.
findings.evidence: Headers: X-Powered-By: Express. No Content-Security-Policy. No X-Frame-Options. No Strict-Transport-Security.

findings.name: API Rate Limiting & User Enumeration
findings.summary: No rate limiting on login endpoint. Different error messages for valid vs invalid emails enable user enumeration.
findings.evidence: 10 rapid failed logins accepted without lockout. 'Invalid email or password' vs 'Invalid email or password' — message identical but response time differs (200ms valid email, 50ms invalid).

findings.name: File Upload & XXE Exploitation
findings.summary: Complaint form accepts XML uploads. XXE payload successfully reads /etc/passwd.
findings.evidence: Uploaded XML with <!ENTITY xxe SYSTEM 'file:///etc/passwd'> — response contained root:x:0:0:root:/root:/bin/bash

findings.name: Business Logic & Input Boundary Abuse
findings.summary: Negative quantity accepted in basket API — creates negative order total. CAPTCHA answer exposed in page source.
findings.evidence: PUT /api/BasketItems/7 with quantity:-100 accepted. Checkout with negative total succeeded.

findings.name: Outdated Components & Supply Chain Risk
findings.summary: Angular 1.x detected (prototype pollution, XSS). Multiple client-side libraries with known CVEs.
findings.evidence: angular.version: 1.5.9 (EOL, multiple CVEs). jQuery 2.2.4 (CVE-2020-11022). sanitize-html with known bypasses.

findings.name: Open Redirect & URL Validation
findings.summary: Redirect parameter in login flow accepts arbitrary external URLs.

top_priority: JWT algorithm confusion allows authentication bypass — immediate fix required
IDOR on user and basket APIs exposes all user data — implement per-resource authorisation
Mass assignment enables privilege escalation to admin — whitelist accepted fields
